text.title: Support Hierarchy: Self-Serve to Proactive
text.summary: The four-level support hierarchy, SLA targets by plan tier, escalation path, and the self-serve-first rule.

rule.statement: Build FAQ and knowledge base before adding any async support channel. 70-85% of support volume can be deflected by a well-built self-serve layer.
rule.rationale: Every ticket that reaches a human could have been answered by documentation. Self-serve assets scale infinitely; human support does not. Building it first prevents backlog from the first day.

rule.statement: If the same question is asked 5+ times, add it to the FAQ immediately. If the same bug is reported repeatedly, prioritize the fix over any other support activity.
rule.rationale: Recurring tickets indicate a product or documentation gap. Answering the same question repeatedly without fixing the root cause is an inefficient use of support time.

example.note: Solopreneur SaaS with 50 knowledge base articles: 70% self-service resolution, 30 tickets/week handled in 5 hours total, 4.7/5 CSAT. Self-serve investment paid back in under 2 months.

rule.statement: Respond to the first ticket from any paying customer within 4 hours regardless of SLA tier. First impressions determine churn risk at the critical early-adoption stage.
rule.rationale: New paying customers form their support experience opinion within the first interaction. A fast first response builds confidence that the product is maintained.

list.item: Paid (high tier): 4 hours + live chat during business hours

section.title: Escalation Path

list.item: Level 2: Standard support (email or async chat)
Level 3: Technical escalation (founder or senior engineer)
Level 4: Emergency (product down, data loss — incident process, not support queue)

rule.statement: Legal, financial, fraud, and data deletion requests must route directly to human queue. Agents must never handle these automatically.
rule.rationale: These topics have regulatory implications (GDPR, PCI-DSS, consumer protection law) that require human judgment and legal accountability.

section.reference: Support policy doc with channels, SLA by plan, and escalation contacts
